Class ResourceService

java.lang.Object
de.hallerweb.enterprise.prioritize.service.resource.ResourceService
@Service @Transactional public class ResourceService extends Object

  • Constructor Details

    • ResourceService

      public ResourceService()

  • Method Details

    • createResourceGroup

      public ResourceGroup createResourceGroup(String name, Department dept, PUser user)

    • getResourceGroupsByDepartment

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public List<ResourceGroup> getResourceGroupsByDepartment(Long departmentId, PUser user)
      Lists the resource groups of a department (for the admin group management screen). Requires Action.READ on the department, mirroring DepartmentService.getDepartmentsByCompany(Long, PUser).

    • renameResourceGroup

      public ResourceGroup renameResourceGroup(Long groupId, String newName, PUser user)
      Renames a resource group (the only editable field). The default group is protected — as with deleteResourceGroup(Long, PUser) it cannot be renamed — and the caller needs Action.UPDATE on it.

    • createResource

      public Resource createResource(Resource resource, Long groupId, PUser user)

    • getResource

      public Resource getResource(Long resourceId, PUser user)

    • getResourcesByGroupId

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public Set<Resource> getResourcesByGroupId(Long groupId)

    • getResourcesInGroup

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public org.springframework.data.domain.Page<ResourceSummaryDTO> getResourcesInGroup(Long groupId, PUser user, org.springframework.data.domain.Pageable pageable)
      Lists a resource group's resources for the admin resources grid, as a page of ResourceSummaryDTO. Requires Action.READ on the group, mirroring the paged DocumentService.getDocumentsInGroup(Long, PUser) precedent. Mapped to a DTO (not the entity) so the rows are safe inside a Vaadin grid's key mapper — Resource's all-fields hashCode would otherwise touch its lazy relations. For each row the currently occupied slots are derived from the reservations overlapping "now", exactly as getResource(Long, PUser) does for a single resource.

    • countResourcesInGroup

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public long countResourcesInGroup(Long groupId, PUser user)
      Counts the resources in a group for the lazy data provider. Same Action.READ check on the group as getResourcesInGroup(Long, PUser, Pageable).

    • getReservationsForResourceDTO

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public List<ResourceReservationDTO> getReservationsForResourceDTO(Long resourceId, PUser user)
      Returns a resource's reservations (past, active and future) flattened to ResourceReservationDTO for the admin occupancy overview. Mapping happens inside the read-only transaction so the lazy reservedBy/timespan are resolved here and the view never touches detached relations. Same Action.READ permission as getReservationsForResource(Long, PUser).

    • reserveResource

      public ResourceReservation reserveResource(Long resourceId, PUser user, Instant from, Instant until)

    • getMyActiveReservations

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public List<ResourceReservation> getMyActiveReservations(Long resourceId, PUser user)
      Returns the reservations active at the point in time now that the given user holds on the resource. This is exactly the set from which the slot is derived when sending a control command: empty → no command possible (409); exactly one → its slot; multiple → ambiguous (409).
      Parameters:
      resourceId - resource
      user - the querying user (also the owner of the reservations)
      Returns:
      active reservations of this user on this resource

    • getReservationsForResource

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public List<ResourceReservation> getReservationsForResource(Long resourceId, PUser user)
      Returns all reservations of a resource (past, active and future) as an occupancy overview. Requires read permission on the resource.
      Parameters:
      resourceId - resource
      user - the querying user (permission check)
      Returns:
      all reservations of the resource

    • cancelReservation

      public void cancelReservation(Integer reservationId, PUser user)
      Cancels (deletes) a reservation and thereby releases the occupied slot.

      Permission: the caller must either be the owner of the reservation OR hold Action.UPDATE on the associated resource (managers may release others' reservations). Consistent with the convention: check in the service, enforcement via exception.

      Parameters:
      reservationId - reservation to cancel
      user - the executing user
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if the reservation does not exist
      org.springframework.security.access.AccessDeniedException - if the user is neither the owner nor has UPDATE rights

    • isResourceAvailable

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public boolean isResourceAvailable(Long resourceId, Instant from, Instant until)

    • deleteResourceGroup

      public void deleteResourceGroup(Long groupId, PUser user)
      Deletes a resource group. * @param groupId ID of the group to delete
      Parameters:
      user - The executing user (for the permission check)

    • deleteResource

      public void deleteResource(Long resourceId, PUser user)
      Deletes a single resource and all reservations associated with it. * @param resourceId ID of the resource
      Parameters:
      user - The executing user

    • partialUpdateResource

      public Resource partialUpdateResource(Long id, Resource patch, PUser user)

    • validateResourceInGroup

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public void validateResourceInGroup(Long resourceId, Long groupId)
      Validates whether a resource belongs to the given resource group. Throws EntityNotFoundException if the resource or group does not exist, IllegalArgumentException if the resource does not belong to the group.
      Parameters:
      resourceId - ID of the resource
      groupId - ID of the resource group

    • setMqttResourceStatusByUuid

      public void setMqttResourceStatusByUuid(String mqttUuid, boolean online)
      Sets the online/offline status of an MQTT resource by its MQTT UUID. Called by the inbound path (device → system, STATUS message). Additionally updates the last-ping timestamp. Unknown UUIDs are ignored (logged), since devices register themselves via discovery first.
      Parameters:
      mqttUuid - the MQTT UUID of the reporting device
      online - new online state

    • recordMqttValueByUuid

      public void recordMqttValueByUuid(String mqttUuid, String name, String value)
      Records a telemetry value reported by an MQTT device (VALUE message). The value is appended to the named data point's comma-separated history (NameValueEntry), which is created on first sight. Only the most recent MAX_VALUE_HISTORY readings are kept. Also refreshes the last-ping timestamp. Unknown UUIDs are ignored (logged), like STATUS, since devices register themselves via discovery first.
      Parameters:
      mqttUuid - the MQTT UUID of the reporting device
      name - the data point name (e.g. temp)
      value - the reported value

    • recordMqttValue

      public Resource recordMqttValue(Long resourceId, String name, String value, PUser user)
      Records a telemetry value for a resource addressed by its numeric id. This is the REST ingest path, used by devices that do not speak MQTT (and by manual/Bruno calls). Unlike recordMqttValueByUuid(String, String, String) it enforces Action.UPDATE permission on the resource; the history handling is identical.
      Parameters:
      resourceId - the id of the target resource
      name - the data point name (e.g. temp)
      value - the reported value
      user - the authenticated user performing the ingest
      Returns:
      the updated resource
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if no resource with the given id exists
      org.springframework.security.access.AccessDeniedException - if the user lacks UPDATE permission on the resource
